Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Look up Lauren Mitchell's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Lauren Mitchell"). Finds her email address: lauren.mitchell@gmail.com. Important: two Lauren Mitchell contacts exist, contacts_contact_273c7d1ff661 (gmail.com, the borrower) and contacts_contact_cebdab0c87b7 (fairviewhomes.com, a realtor). The agent must identify the borrower's Gmail address.
2. Look up Taylor Morgan's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Taylor Morgan"). Finds her email address: taylor.morgan@mail.com.
3. Search emails for Lauren Mitchell correspondence using search_emails (query: "Lauren Mitchell" or "Mitchell appraisal" or similar). Expected findings: email from Lauren Mitchell (lauren.mitchell@gmail.com) titled "Recent sales near my house" sending 3 recent comparable sales; email from appraiser Michelle Tran (mtran@carolinaappraisal.com) titled "RE: Recent sales near the Mitchell property" declining to revise the value; Denise Holloway's email titled "Mitchell ROV documentation" sent to Amy and Elena recommending framing as a reconsideration of value package rather than a formal complaint; Hana Kim's reply titled "RE: Next steps on the appraisal" noting a second appraisal would be a $475 vendor bill.
4. Search emails for Taylor Morgan correspondence using search_emails (query: "Taylor Morgan" or "gift funds" or similar). Expected findings: Amy's email to Taylor explaining the need for a donor bank statement showing a $20K withdrawal; Taylor's reply saying parents refuse to share their bank statement.
5. Search Slack for discussions about Mitchell and Morgan situations using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mitchell" or "Morgan" or "gift" or "appraisal" or similar). Expected findings: discussion in #loan-processing (C002) about Taylor Morgan's gift fund situation (LN-2026-00631) and about Lauren Mitchell's appraisal challenge (LN-2026-00627). Note: LN-2026-00631 and LN-2026-00627 are not assigned to Amy in the LOS; they belong to other staff. Amy is involved via email and Slack, reflecting her senior role managing client relationships.
6. Search CRM for Stephanie Boyd using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Stephanie Boyd"). Finds her CRM contact record (ID: ae432d04397244ccb9b8eb524016fada, email: stephanie.boyd@icloud.com, company: Ivester Jackson Christie's, jobtitle: Realtor). Check for any existing engagement notes on the relationship (e.g., crm_list_engagements with contact_ids including that id).
7. Search emails for Stephanie Boyd correspondence using search_emails (query: "Stephanie Boyd" or "Boyd" or "stephanie.boyd" or similar). Expected findings: email from Stephanie to Amy (email_email_sb0407fha2026) titled "Quick question on FHA timelines" dated April 7, 2026, asking about FHA processing timelines and mentioning a new couple in Ballantyne, Amy never replied (~three weeks of silence). Also: substantial email traffic between Stephanie Boyd and Sofia Reyes (processor) about active deals - including threads on Brennan, Sardis Rd, and seller's-agent follow-ups - showing Stephanie is actively sending business to Keystone while much of the day-to-day coordination has run through processing rather than Amy.
8. Search CRM for Lauren Mitchell and Taylor Morgan engagement history: run crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Lauren Mitchell") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Taylor Morgan") as separate calls (full_name is the required parameter). Finds their CRM records and existing engagements before creating new ones. Two Lauren Mitchell CRM rows exist (borrower gmail.com vs Fairview Homes coordinator); use the borrower (gmail.com) record for loan-related engagements.
9. Check loan status for Mitchell and Morgan files via LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to check the current status of Lauren Mitchell's and Taylor Morgan's loans. This provides the factual basis for the status update emails to the borrowers.
10. Check Stripe for fee issues on Mitchell and Morgan files using stripe_search_charges or stripe_list_charges to check for any unusual fees or charges on these borrowers' loans, as the prompt asks to flag fee concerns.
11. Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement for Lauren Mitchell's situation, Taylor Morgan's situation, and Stephanie Boyd's partner relationship review. The prompt says "update our records with notes showing I've caught up on all three situations." The agent must infer that "our records" means the CRM.
12. Send status update email to Lauren Mitchell using send_email (sender: "amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["lauren.mitchell@gmail.com"], subject relating to appraisal status update, content covering: appraiser declined revision, ROV package path per Denise Holloway's compliance guidance).
13. Send status update email to Taylor Morgan using send_email (sender: "amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["taylor.morgan@mail.com"], subject relating to gift fund documentation, content covering: understanding parents' concern about bank statement, next steps).
14. Send check-in email to Stephanie Boyd using reply_to_email on email_email_sb0407fha2026 (Stephanie's unanswered April 7 email) or send_email to stephanie.boyd@icloud.com. Content covers: responding to her FHA timelines question, acknowledging the delayed response, and reaffirming the partnership. Using reply_to_email is natural here since the prompt says "responding to her message."
15. Post relationship summary in Slack #sales-pipeline using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: string body summarizing Lauren and Taylor client situations, Stephanie Boyd partner relationship status, and where key relationships stand; optional content_type: "text/markdown").

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

6 The final response mentions that the appraiser on the Lauren Mitchell file declined or refused to revise the appraised value.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response clearly states: 'Appraiser Michelle Tran rejected the ROV attempt — she said Lauren's 3 comps were outside her market area or post-effective date.' This directly references the appraiser's refusal to revise.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'appraiser Michelle Tran refused to revise' and the Lauren Mitchell section states 'Lauren sent you 3 comps; appraiser Michelle Tran refused to revise'.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states: 'The original appraiser (Michelle Tran at Carolina Appraisal) refused to revise.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'You forwarded them to appraiser Michelle Tran, who refused the revision (comps outside market area / post-effective date closings).'
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Appraiser Michelle Tran refused the ROV — rejected Lauren's 3 Waverly Ridge comps (outside market area / post-effective date).'
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states: 'Appraiser Michelle Tran refused to revise — said your 3 comps were outside her market area or closed after effective date.'
7 The final response mentions that Taylor Morgan's parents are refusing or reluctant to provide their bank statement for the $20,000 gift fund documentation (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states: 'Taylor's parents gifted $20K (gift letter on file), but they refuse to provide a bank statement.' This clearly reports the core issue.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'Parents refused citing privacy; you sent a follow-up suggesting redaction — no response' under Taylor Morgan's section.
